raw_data.note: Side effects reported as multiple categories (nausea, vomiting, flushing, drowsiness, pain). Combined any-side-effect counts: Serial IV Bolus arm: nausea 10, vomiting 5, flushing 18, drowsiness 15, pain 28 (not mutually exclusive, total unique not derivable). Unacceptable/very unacceptable: 4/98 (4.0%) serial bolus vs 2/98 (2.0%) continuous infusion, P=0.68. Individual side effect counts not combined into single binary outcome in paper.

source_quote: Table 5 Side Effects: Serial IV Bolus (n=98): Nausea 10 (10.2%), Vomiting 5 (5.1%), Flushing 18 (18.4%), Drowsiness 15 (15.3%), Pain 28 (28.6%). Continuous Infusion (n=98): Nausea 8 (8.2%), Vomiting 2 (2.0%), Flushing 9 (9.2%), Drowsiness 13 (13.2%), Pain 9 (9.2%). Unacceptable/Very unacceptable: 4 (4.0%) vs 2 (2.0%), P=0.68.

In the serial IV bolus arm, 8 women stopped treatment early due to side effects (n=4), signs of toxicity (n=2), fetal distress (n=1) or provider error (n=1). In the continuous infusion arm, 6 women stopped treatment early due to delivery (n=3), low urinary output (n=2) or provider request (n=1).

raw_data.note: Post-treatment values. Table reports Mean +/- SE (not SD). SEG (Schroth exercise, experimental) post: 12.0+/-4.7; PEG (Pilates exercise, control) post: 16.0+/-6.9. Pre-test values: SEG 23.6+/-1.5, PEG 24.0+/-2.6. Both groups showed significant intragroup change (p<0.05). Intergroup comparison showed SEG had significantly greater Cobb angle reduction than PEG (p<0.05).

source_quote: Table 2. Comparison of the Cobb angle and weight distribution within and between groups: Cobb angle - PEG (n=12): Pre-test 24.0+/-2.6, Post-test 16.0+/-6.9*; SEG (n=12): Pre-test 23.6+/-1.5, Post-test 12.0+/-4.7*a. Mean +/- SE. *Significant difference from pre-test, p<0.05. aSignificant difference in gains between the two groups, p<0.05.

outcome: Pain (100-mm VAS): multistrand StSt versus superelastic nickel-titanium (NiTi)

raw_data.note: Repeated-measures design with 32 time points. Mixed-effects model found no significant overall group difference (F=2.65, df=92.6, P=0.1071). Significant Group x Time interaction (F=1.57, df=2501, P=0.0226). NiTi had significantly higher pain at 12h (estimate=-2.60, SE=1.11, P=0.0193), Day 1 morning (estimate=-2.46, P=0.0273), Day 1 afternoon (estimate=-2.35, P=0.0346), Day 1 bedtime (estimate=-2.26, P=0.042). Peak pain at Day 1 morning: NiTi 28.8+/-11.0 vs StSt 26.4+/-9.0. Raw VAS scores available for all 32 time points in Table 2.

source_quote: No statistically significant difference was found for overall pain [F value=2.65, degrees of freedom (df)=92.6; P=0.1071]. However, compared to multistranded stainless steel wires, pain in subjects with superelastic nickel-titanium archwires was significantly greater at 12 h (t=2.34; P=0.0193), as well as at day 1 in the morning (t=2.21, P=0.0273), afternoon (t=2.11, P=0.0346) and at bedtime (t=2.03, P=0.042).
